spec: F2 harness RunSpec — verifier-offload on definitional QA (HE1 primary; HE2 cascade, HC3 PRM, HS12 placement riders). Frozen design: registry/experiments/f2.json (frozen_sha256 28874f2baa8573ed0d4948bf01af3ae10158560384e236fa0772aaf0cfaa56ec) + ops amendments 1-2. This manifest pins the harness INPUTS only; it may not change any frozen design field or threshold.

pins.perItemRecordSha256: every d-qa item carries record_path + record_sha256; the verifier re-hashes the canonical record file at use and fails closed (ERR_RECORD_PIN) on mismatch

prm.note: HC3/EF3 trained-PRM comparator (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B-Instruct backbone + PRM head, Qwen2ForPrmModel; the small PRM of Liu et al. arXiv:2502.06703). Checkpoint ships trust_remote_code custom classes (pinned by this revision sha) and pytorch_model.bin only (no safetensors). The real-path PRM backend is still to build — the runner keeps its ERR_PRM_TODO fail-closed stub; mock uses StubPRM

design_constants_from_frozen_record.note: copied VERBATIM from registry/experiments/f2.json for runner convenience; the frozen record is authoritative and prereg-freeze lint compares these

arm_plan.self_consistency_note: N=5 votes = the FLOP envelope of the max retry budget k=4 (1 greedy + 4 resamples); fixed BEFORE any final run — never chosen after seeing results; realized FLOPs land on the F0 ledger and analysis/f2.py compares ledgers, not intentions
arm_plan.prm_note: best-of-2 reranking ~ FLOP envelope of verify at k=1 (2 generations + 2 PRM forwards vs 2 generations + CPU verify); realized FLOPs on the ledger
arm_plan.retry_final_answer: last attempt (accepted or budget-exhausted); pre-declared, no post-hoc pick
arm_plan.cascade_budget_rule: escalate gate-flagged items in ascending small-model-confidence order up to ceil(budget * n_items); measured p_escalate goes on the ledger
arm_plan.logprob_gate_rule: escalate the lowest-confidence fraction b of items (budget-quantile threshold on the small model's answer-margin; FrugalGPT-style)
arm_plan.shared_affordance: P10 IF-C constrained decoding for EVERY arm: answers are produced by constrained selection over the pinned option set (A-D or yes/no) via sequence logprob; arms differ ONLY in verification instrument and context block, never in output affordances

prompt_frames.qa_prefix: Answer the following question about the meaning of a word.

flop_accounting.formula: F0 section 3.3: FLOPs = 2*N_active*T_total + 2*L*T^2*d_attn per sequence; prefill and decode metered separately; T measured with the arm's own tokenizer
flop_accounting.cpu_note: CPU-side verifier work is metered as measured wall-clock seconds and converted at this pinned rate ONLY for the aggregate figure; wall-clock is reported separately (F0 section 3.3)
flop_accounting.usd_note: Modal list prices used for planning + $-per-query conversion; F0 section 3.5 AWS pins (T4 0.53, A10G 1.01) retained in dry-plan output for cross-reference; actual Modal billing is reconciled from the dashboard at run time

planning.note: dry-plan ESTIMATES only — never emitted as measurements

mock.note: SYNTHETIC mechanics-only stub LM (no torch needed); every number it produces is labelled MOCK and never a measurement; the verifier, extraction, gates, flop-meter and emitter code paths are the REAL ones

inputs_status.d-xif: BUILT + pinned (amendment 5) — 500 covered + 150 control real model outputs per rung (R1/R2), gate PASS at both rungs (0/500 failures, Wilson-LB 0.0000 <= 0.10); IF-1 fork decided: IF-C STANDS; real runs re-verify the labelled set mechanically (run_extraction_instrument_dxif) and fail closed on the pins above
inputs_status.d-ext: BUILT + pinned (amendment 4) — 500 M0b-filtered OpenBookQA items (RT-7a; WiC unavailable, preference rank 2); real runs load items/external.jsonl for item_correct_external and fail closed on the pins above
inputs_status.if1_fork: DECIDED 2026-07-08 pre-final-phase (P10 section 3): IF-C constrained decoding STANDS as the model<->record interface — format-tax h_degradation -0.021 (R1) / -0.708 (R2), both fail the >= 0.2 firing bar (the constraint HELPS); IF-A F1 0.824/0.919 < 0.95; record: data/d-xif/if1-fork.json (inside the pinned corpus bytes)
